channel.title: The Friendship Practice — Guides
channel.description: Practical guides on friendship by Carole Stromboni.

item.title: Why male friendships fade after 30
item.description: Male friendships don't end in a fight. They fade through distance, cancelled plans, and the slow disappearance of shared contexts. Here's what the research shows.

item.title: How men can rebuild friendships without making it awkward
item.description: You don't need a deep conversation or a vulnerability retreat. You need a recurring activity, one invitation, and a follow-up. Here's how to rebuild.

item.title: Friendship quotes: eight counter-intuitive observations from a friendship coach
item.description: Eight observations on adult friendship by Carole Stromboni, friendship coach: on following up, loneliness, repetition, and what actually builds closeness.

item.title: How to make friends as a new parent
item.description: New parenthood is isolating, and polite coffee dates aren't the answer. Here is how to find other parents who get it and build real connections fast.

item.title: How to keep work friendships after going remote
item.description: When you go remote, you lose the hallway moments that kept work friendships alive. Here is how to replace that structure before the friendships quietly fade.

item.title: How to make new friends after a friendship ends
item.description: Losing a close friend leaves a real void. Clearing out a broken connection makes room for something healthier. Here is how to rebuild with confidence.

item.title: How to follow up after meeting someone
item.description: Afraid to text someone you just met in case you seem too eager? Research shows people like us more than we think. Here is how to follow up without the stress.

item.title: Why do friendships fade?
item.description: Friendships rarely end in a big fight. They drift away when nobody makes the next plan. Here is why it happens and how to catch it before it is too late.

item.title: How to turn acquaintances into friends
item.description: How do you cross from someone you know to someone you trust? It takes more than hello. Here is what actually moves an acquaintance into a real friend.

item.title: How to make friends as an expat
item.description: Making friends as an expat is a real challenge: no existing network, different cultural scripts, a ticking clock. Here is what actually works abroad.

item.title: How to know if someone wants to be your friend
item.description: Reading the signals of mutual interest is one of the hardest parts of adult friendship. A practical guide to recognizing when a connection has real potential.

item.title: What is friendship coaching?
item.description: Friendship coaching isn't therapy. It is a step-by-step strategy for your social life, like a personal trainer for your friendships.

item.title: How to Stay Friends With Someone Who Just Had a Baby
item.description: When your friend has a baby, their free time drops to zero overnight. If you want to keep the bond, you have to change the rules. Here is how.

item.title: What to Do When a Friendship Feels One-Sided
item.description: Are you the only one making plans, sending texts, and checking in? Here is how to spot a one-sided friendship and decide what to do about it.

item.title: How to Make Friends When You Work From Home
item.description: Remote work removes the easiest structure for repeated contact in adult life. Here is how to build a real social life when home is also your office.

item.title: How to introduce friends to each other
item.description: A good introduction is about preparation, not spontaneity. Here is who to ask first, what to say, and when to step back and let the friendship form on its own.

item.title: When a friendship ends
item.description: Friendship breakups can hurt as much as romantic ones, but nobody talks about them. Here is how to navigate the grief, accept the closure, and heal.

item.title: How to meet people in a new city
item.description: Meeting people in a new city runs on repetition, not luck. Here is how to create the repeated contact that turns early acquaintances into real friendships.

item.title: How to be a better friend
item.description: Being a better friend is less about grand gestures and more about reliable small ones: following up, showing up, and being easy to reach when it matters.

item.title: How to make friends after 30
item.description: After 30, friendship doesn't stop being possible. It stops being automatic. Here is how to build it deliberately without pretending adult life works like college.

item.title: How to make friends as an adult
item.description: It takes roughly 50 hours of meaningful shared time to move from stranger to casual friend, and 200 for a close one. Here is how to make those hours happen.

item.title: How to reconnect with old friends
item.description: The story you tell yourself before you send the message is always harder than the message itself. Here is how to reconnect without guilt or a long explanation.

item.title: Why making friends as an adult is hard
item.description: Adulthood didn't kill your social skills. It destroyed your infrastructure. Here is why adult friendship requires deliberate design, not just more willpower.

item.title: How to keep friends as an adult
item.description: Adult friendships don't end in fights. They end in drift. Here is how to maintain them through consistent small acts, not grand gestures or perfect timing.
